title: carum caucasicum
intro: Carum caucasicum, also known as Carum carvi, is a perennial herb native to Europe, Western Asia, and the Caucasus region. It is a member of the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is commonly known as wild dill or chervil. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative properties, which make it effective in treating digestive issues and relieving nausea. The health benefits of Carum caucasicum include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce flatulence, and soothe stomach ulcers, and it is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures.
